description_long: This is a robust, metallic helmet featuring a dark, weathered bronze finish and two large, asymmetrical horns extending from either side. The front of the helmet has a pronounced vertical ridge running from the top to the nose guard, which splits into two downward prongs to protect the face. The horns are textured and curve outward, with one horn significantly longer and more upwardly curved than the other, giving the helmet a distinctive, intimidating appearance. The helmet's surface is slightly reflective, with subtle dents and marks suggesting battle wear. There are no visible ventilation holes, logos, or graphic designs, and the interior appears hollow to accommodate a human head. The overall design is reminiscent of Viking or fantasy warrior helmets, intended for dramatic visual impact rather than modern safety standards.
description: A dark bronze, battle-worn helmet with two large, unevenly curved horns and a prominent nose guard. The surface is metallic and slightly reflective, with no visible graphics or ventilation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the helmet's face opening, central ridge, and both horns, with the right horn curving sharply upward and the left horn extending outward.
description_view_1: Left rear angle highlights the long, upward-curving right horn and the smooth, rounded back of the helmet.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the full span of both horns and the helmet's rounded, seamless back surface.
description_view_3: Right rear angle emphasizes the shorter, outward-pointing left horn and the helmet's metallic texture.
